rationale: Completeness/judgment-preservation lens on 13788634. (a) MECHANIZED prose GONE, both mirrors: ms-panel-run's mkdir + hand-typed panel.json schema + round/SHA co-bump prose + the skill-authored '## Your job' verdict-JSON template; ms-panel-tally's Load-verdicts loop + Tabulate step + decision-matrix table. (b) JUDGMENT sections KEPT and INTACT: ms-panel-run keeps Launch the panel, Codex failure detection, Working directory matters, Slot lens defaults, Anti-patterns (all byte-identical to parent — diff only touched Step 0 + one anti-pattern bullet). ms-panel-tally keeps Consolidate (dedup+ranking a/b/c/d), Artifact gates (full table + lola-f4a8 $417 postmortem), After a halt — recovery (inventory/classify/stale/abandon), Escape hatch (MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL), Anti-patterns (5 bullets). Every invariant I hunted for has a home: co-bump preserved+mechanized; REJECT-halts reproduced in binary (gate.go:243, tally.go:206, 'no vote count overrides'); abandon procedure, codex-failure fallback, artifact-gate HARD block, family-split note all survive. One soft methodology line dropped without a home (finding below) — non-blocking, and re-adding it would fight the spec's single-verdict-instruction invariant.

findings.note: Non-blocking gap: the old skill-authored '## Your job' BRIEF block carried round>=2 reviewer methodology — 'evaluate cold (round 1) vs verify prior asks (round>=2)' and 'Each ask -> ADDRESSED / PARTIAL / MISSED / NEW_ISSUE'. The plan correctly removes that block (verdict contract is now binary-owned in renderBriefHeader), but the binary's fixed, non-panel-specific '## Your job' header does NOT reproduce the per-ask disposition taxonomy, and neither does the thinned skill's new Prior-Round Asks stub guidance. A round-2 reviewer now gets a bare 'Prior-Round Asks' list with no instruction on how to classify each. This is soft methodology, not a gate/safety invariant, so I do not block — and re-authoring it in the skill would re-introduce the second verdict-instruction surface spec 110 exists to eliminate. Right home if preserved: the binary stub or a one-line note under the skill's Prior-Round Asks stub-fill step (not a verdict block). Candidate follow-up bead, not a fix for fbel.5.

Minor nuance softened but instruction preserved: old Tabulate said 'Family asymmetry matters'; new step 3 keeps the family-split reporting task (APPROVEs among R1-R3 claude vs R4-R6 codex) and Anti-patterns still says 'note family asymmetry'. Instruction survives; only the emphatic phrasing is gone. Acceptable.
